key_sentences.chinese: 人生中面临选择时，既要考虑现实，也不能完全放弃理想。
key_sentences.pinyin: Rén shēng zhōng miàn lín xuǎn zé shí, jì yào kǎo lǜ xiàn shí, yě bù néng wán quán fàng qì lǐ xiǎng.
key_sentences.english: When facing choices in life, you must consider reality while also not completely giving up on your ideals.

key_sentences.chinese: 既然已经做了决定，就不要再犹豫，勇敢地走下去。
key_sentences.pinyin: Jì rán yǐ jīng zuò le jué dìng, jiù bú yào zài yóu yù, yǒng gǎn de zǒu xià qù.
key_sentences.english: Since you've already made the decision, don't hesitate anymore — move forward bravely.

key_sentences.chinese: 我宁可放弃高薪的待遇，也不愿意放弃自己的目标和理想。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ nìng kě fàng qì gāo xīn de dài yù, yě bú yuàn yì fàng qì zì jǐ de mù biāo hé lǐ xiǎng.
key_sentences.english: I would rather give up a high salary and good benefits than give up my goals and ideals.

key_sentences.chinese: 好的机会不等人，错过了很可能后悔莫及。
key_sentences.pinyin: Hǎo de jī huì bù děng rén, cuò guò le hěn kě néng hòu huǐ mò jí.
key_sentences.english: Good opportunities wait for no one — miss them and you may regret it for the rest of your life.

key_sentences.chinese: 尽管前途未知，他还是选择了自己喜欢的行业，坚持着那份勇气。
key_sentences.pinyin: Jǐn guǎn qián tú wèi zhī, tā hái shì xuǎn zé le zì jǐ xǐ huān de háng yè, jiān chí zhe nà fèn yǒng qì.
key_sentences.english: Even though the future was uncertain, he still chose the industry he loved, holding on to that courage.

key_sentences.chinese: 为了选择哪条人生道路发愁是正常的，但不能一直犹豫不决。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wèi le xuǎn zé nǎ tiáo rén shēng dào lù fā chóu shì zhèng cháng de, dàn bù néng yī zhí yóu yù bù jué.
key_sentences.english: It is normal to worry about which path in life to choose, but you cannot remain indecisive forever.

grammar_points.title: 既然…就 — Since (that's the case)…then
grammar_points.explanation: 既然…就 is used when acknowledging an established fact or situation and drawing a logical conclusion from it. Structure: 既然 + [acknowledged fact/condition], (Subject +) 就 + [logical conclusion/action]. The first clause introduces something already true or decided; the second clause presents what naturally follows. This pattern is stronger than 因为…所以 because it implies the situation is already fixed and the speaker is simply acting on it. 既然你已经做了决定，就不要再犹豫了 - Since you've already made the decision, don't hesitate anymore.

examples.chinese: 既然你已经做了决定，就不要再犹豫了。
examples.pinyin: Jì rán nǐ yǐ jīng zuò le jué dìng, jiù bú yào zài yóu yù le.
examples.english: Since you've already made the decision, don't hesitate anymore.

examples.chinese: 既然这份工作的待遇那么好，你就应该好好珍惜。
examples.pinyin: Jì rán zhè fèn gōng zuò de dài yù nà me hǎo, nǐ jiù yīng gāi hǎo hǎo zhēn xī.
examples.english: Since this job's benefits are so good, you should treasure it.

exercises.question: _____ 你已经设定了目标，就应该全力以赴，不要半途放弃。
既然他已经后悔了，_____ 给他一次改正的机会吧。
既然这次机会这么难得，_____ 不要犹豫，赶快把握住。
Which sentence uses 既然…就 correctly?
exercises.correct: 既然你不喜欢这份工作，就早点换一个吧。
options: 既然你不喜欢这份工作，就早点换一个吧。
既然你不喜欢这份工作，但还是继续做下去吧。
虽然你不喜欢这份工作，就早点换一个吧。
既然你不喜欢这份工作，才换一个吧。

exercises.question: 既然你选择了坚持理想，_____ 要做好面对困难的准备。

mini_exercises.question: 既然...就... differs from 如果...就... in that...
mini_exercises.correct: 既然 refers to a fact already established or accepted; 如果 introduces a hypothetical condition
options: 既然 refers to a fact already established or accepted; 如果 introduces a hypothetical condition
They are completely interchangeable in all contexts
既然 is only used in formal writing; 如果 is informal
既然 expresses surprise; 如果 expresses a condition

options: 既然选择了这条路，就要坚持走下去。
选择了这条路既然，就要坚持走下去。
既然就选择了这条路，要坚持走下去。
就既然选择了，这条路要坚持走下去。
option_pinyin: jì rán xuǎn zé le zhè tiáo lù jiù yào jiān chí zǒu xià qù
xuǎn zé le zhè tiáo lù jì rán jiù yào jiān chí zǒu xià qù
jì rán jiù xuǎn zé le zhè tiáo lù yào jiān chí zǒu xià qù
jiù jì rán xuǎn zé le zhè tiáo lù yào jiān chí zǒu xià qù

grammar_points.title: 宁可…也不 — Would rather…than
grammar_points.explanation: 宁可…也不 expresses a preference for one option over another, even if the preferred option involves some sacrifice or difficulty. Structure: Subject + 宁可 + [preferred but possibly harder option] + 也不 + [rejected option]. The speaker actively chooses hardship over an easier but undesirable alternative. 我宁可放弃高薪工作，也不愿意放弃自己的理想 - I would rather give up a high-paying job than give up my ideals.

examples.chinese: 我宁可放弃高薪工作，也不愿意放弃自己的理想。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ nìng kě fàng qì gāo xīn gōng zuò, yě bú yuàn yì fàng qì zì jǐ de lǐ xiǎng.
examples.english: I would rather give up a high-paying job than give up my ideals.

examples.chinese: 她宁可每天发愁，也不愿意在一份没有意义的工作上浪费时间。
examples.pinyin: Tā nìng kě měi tiān fā chóu, yě bú yuàn yì zài yī fèn méi yǒu yì yì de gōng zuò shàng làng fèi shí jiān.
examples.english: She would rather worry every day than waste her time on a meaningless job.

exercises.question: 他宁可选择待遇差的工作，_____ 愿意在不稳定的公司受委屈。

options: 我宁可咬牙坚持，也不轻易放弃目标。
我宁可咬牙坚持，所以不轻易放弃目标。
我宁可咬牙坚持，就不轻易放弃目标。
我宁可咬牙坚持，但不轻易放弃目标。

他宁可放弃成功的机会，_____ 违背自己的良心。

mini_exercises.question: 宁可...也不... expresses...
mini_exercises.correct: A strong preference: 'would rather do A than do B' — A may be difficult but is preferred over B
options: A strong preference: 'would rather do A than do B' — A may be difficult but is preferred over B
A concession: even though A is true, B still happens
A condition: only if A, then B is possible
A sequence: A happens, and then B follows

mini_exercises.question: Which sentence correctly uses 宁可…也不?
mini_exercises.correct: 他宁可一个人奋斗，也不愿意放弃目标。
options: 他宁可一个人奋斗，也不愿意放弃目标。
他也不宁可一个人奋斗，愿意放弃目标。
宁可他一个人奋斗，也不愿意放弃目标。
他一个人奋斗宁可，也不愿意放弃目标。
option_pinyin: tā nìng kě yī gè rén fèn dòu yě bù yuàn yì fàng qì mù biāo
tā yě bù nìng kě yī gè rén fèn dòu yuàn yì fàng qì mù biāo
nìng kě tā yī gè rén fèn dòu yě bù yuàn yì fàng qì mù biāo
tā yī gè rén fèn dòu nìng kě yě bù yuàn yì fàng qì mù biāo

grammar_points.title: 各自 — Each; Respectively; On one's own
grammar_points.explanation: 各自 is an adverb meaning 'each one individually' or 'respectively.' Structure: Subject + 各自 + Verb. It emphasizes that different people or parties each perform an action separately or independently, rather than together. Unlike 分别 (which can describe actions done at different times), 各自 highlights that each party has their own distinct path, situation, or responsibility. 他们各自做出了不同的选择 — They each made their own different choices.

examples.chinese: 毕业后，他们各自走上了不同的前途之路，但都一辈子没有忘记彼此。
examples.pinyin: Bì yè hòu, tā men gè zì zǒu shàng le bù tóng de qián tú zhī lù, dàn dōu yī bèi zi méi yǒu wàng jì bǐ cǐ.
examples.english: After graduation, they each went their own way toward their futures, but none of them ever forgot each other their whole lives.

examples.chinese: 两人充分讨论之后，各自提出了对前途的盼望和规划。
examples.pinyin: Liǎng rén chōng fèn tǎo lùn zhī hòu, gè zì tí chū le duì qián tú de pàn wàng hé guī huà.
examples.english: After thorough discussion, the two each put forward their own hopes and plans for the future.

exercises.question: 毕业后，他们_____走上了不同的发展道路，各自追求一辈子的盼望。
讨论结束后，他们_____回到自己的工作岗位，全力以赴地完成各自的任务。
两人对前途的盼望方向不同，因此决定_____去追求各自的选择。
Which sentence uses 各自 correctly?
exercises.correct: 他们各自有不同的前途规划，不需要为对方的选择发愁。
options: 他们各自有不同的前途规划，不需要为对方的选择发愁。
他们各自一起决定了同一件事。
各自他们都选择了同一条路。
他们互相各自帮助对方做出选择。

exercises.question: 面对人生中的重要选择，每个人都需要_____做出判断，勿因他人影响而一辈子后悔。

mini_exercises.question: 各自 emphasizes that...
mini_exercises.correct: Each person/party does something individually or has their own distinct situation — not together
options: Each person/party does something individually or has their own distinct situation — not together
Everyone does the same thing at the same time
Actions happen one after another in a sequence
The result is shared equally among all parties

mini_exercises.question: Fill in: After graduation, they each went their own way.
mini_exercises.question_chinese: 毕业后，他们___走上了不同的前途之路。
mini_exercises.question_pinyin: 毕业后，他们___走上了不同的前途之路。
mini_exercises.question_english: After graduation, they each went their own way.

mini_exercises.question: Which sentence correctly uses 各自?
mini_exercises.correct: 他们各自做出了选择，没有为对方的决定发愁。
options: 他们各自做出了选择，没有为对方的决定发愁。
他们各自一起讨论了同一个问题。
各自就是他们喜欢的方式。
他们各自都犹豫地选择了同一条路。
option_pinyin: tā men gè zì zuò chū le xuǎn zé méi yǒu wèi duì fāng de jué dìng fā chóu
tā men gè zì yī qǐ tǎo lùn le tóng yī gè wèn tí
gè zì jiù shì tā men xǐ huān de fāng shì
tā men gè zì dōu yóu yù de xuǎn zé le tóng yī tiáo lù

grammar_points.title: 勿 — Do not / Must not (formal/literary)
grammar_points.explanation: 勿 is a classical Chinese particle meaning 'do not' or 'must not.' It is the formal, literary equivalent of 不要 and appears in written notices, signage, formal announcements, or elevated spoken Chinese. Structure: 勿 + Verb. It creates a prohibition or strong warning with a more authoritative and concise tone than 不要. Common expressions: 请勿打扰 (Please do not disturb), 勿忘初心 (Never forget your original intention).

examples.chinese: 面对重大选择时，勿因一时冲动而一辈子后悔。
examples.pinyin: Miàn duì zhòng dà xuǎn zé shí, wù yīn yī shí chōng dòng ér yī bèi zi hòu huǐ.
examples.english: When facing a major choice, do not act on a moment of impulse and regret it for a lifetime.

examples.chinese: 既然已经盼望多年，就全力以赴，勿在最后一步犹豫退缩。
examples.pinyin: Jì rán yǐ jīng pàn wàng duō nián, jiù quán lì yǐ fù, wù zài zuì hòu yī bù yóu yù tuì suō.
examples.english: Since you have hoped for so many years, give it your all — do not waver or back down at the final step.

exercises.question: ___面对选择时冲动行事，以免一辈子后悔。
既然已经决定辞职，就全力以赴，___再犹豫不前。

word_hints.犹豫不前: to hesitate and not move forward

exercises.question: ___因一时冲动辞职，要认真权衡稳定的待遇与个人前途。
Which sentence uses 勿 correctly?
exercises.correct: 面对发愁的事，勿轻易冲动，要冷静思考。
options: 面对发愁的事，勿轻易冲动，要冷静思考。
他勿了一个冲动的决定。
她一辈子都勿愿意辞职。

exercises.question: 她盼望全力以赴，却总是犹豫，旁人劝她：'___再拖延，机不可失。'

word_hints.机不可失: don't miss the opportunity; opportunity knocks but once

mini_exercises.question: 勿 vs 不要: what is the key difference?
mini_exercises.correct: 勿 is formal/literary (used in notices, written instructions, elevated speech); 不要 is the everyday spoken equivalent
options: 勿 is formal/literary (used in notices, written instructions, elevated speech); 不要 is the everyday spoken equivalent
勿 only applies to other people; 不要 applies to yourself
勿 negates adjectives; 不要 negates verbs
They are completely identical in meaning and usage

mini_exercises.question: Fill in: Do not act impulsively when facing important choices.
mini_exercises.question_chinese: 面对重要选择时，___因冲动而一辈子后悔。
mini_exercises.question_pinyin: 面对重要选择时，___因冲动而一辈子后悔。
mini_exercises.question_english: Do not act impulsively when facing important choices.

mini_exercises.question: Which sentence correctly uses 勿?
mini_exercises.correct: 既然选择了全力以赴，勿再犹豫。
options: 既然选择了全力以赴，勿再犹豫。
option_pinyin: jì rán xuǎn zé le quán lì yǐ fù wù zài yóu yù
tā wù xuǎn zé le cí zhí
wù tā yī bèi zi dōu hòu huǐ le
tā de qián tú wù wěn dìng

culture_notes.title: 纸上谈兵 — The Danger of Theory Without Practice
culture_notes.content:
During the Warring States Period (战国时代), the state of Zhao (赵国) had a general named Zhao She (赵奢) who was a brilliant military strategist. His son, Zhao Kuo (赵括), grew up reading every book on military theory and could discuss battle tactics endlessly. He could defeat his own father in theoretical debate — and yet he had never actually fought in a battle.

In 260 BC, Zhao Kuo was put in command of 400,000 soldiers at the Battle of Changping (长平之战) against the state of Qin (秦国). Confident in his book knowledge, he charged into battle using textbook strategies — but real warfare demanded quick judgment, experience, and adaptability that no book could teach. The result was catastrophic: almost all 400,000 soldiers were killed or surrendered. It was one of the deadliest battles in Chinese history.

The idiom 纸上谈兵 (literally: 'talking strategy on paper') was born from this story. It describes someone who can theorize brilliantly but fails completely when faced with real-world challenges.

This lesson applies directly to facing life's choices: it is not enough to plan, analyze, and deliberate endlessly. At some point, you must act. The only way to truly know if a choice is right is to live it. Even a well-thought-out choice may fail — and an impulsive one may succeed. What matters is having the courage to move from theory into action, and to adjust as you go.

idioms.english: Armchair strategy; to theorize without practical experience

quiz.question: Which word means 'to hesitate or waver between options'?
Complete the sentence: 既然你已经知道答案了，_____ 直接说出来吧。
What does 后悔莫及 mean?
Which sentence uses 宁可…也不 correctly?
quiz.correct: 他宁可失去稳定的工作，也不放弃自己的理想。
options: 他宁可失去稳定的工作，也不放弃自己的理想。
他宁可失去稳定的工作，所以放弃自己的理想。
他宁可失去稳定的工作，就放弃自己的理想。
既然失去稳定的工作，他也不放弃理想。

quiz.question: What is the historical lesson behind the idiom 纸上谈兵?
quiz.correct: Zhao Kuo was skilled in military theory but failed in real battle, losing 400,000 soldiers.
options: Zhao Kuo was skilled in military theory but failed in real battle, losing 400,000 soldiers.
A general who refused to read military books eventually won a great battle.
A student who studied hard succeeded in becoming a top official.
An emperor who ignored his generals lost his kingdom.

quiz.question: 尽管非常发愁，她 _____ 没有放弃坚持目标。

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我现在的工作很稳定，待遇也不错，但是一点都不是我想做的事情。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ xiàn zài de gōng zuò hěn wěn dìng, dài yù yě bú cuò, dàn shì yī diǎn dōu bú shì wǒ xiǎng zuò de shì qíng.
lines.english: My current job is very stable and the benefits are good, but it's not at all what I want to do.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你已经为这个问题发愁多久了？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ yǐ jīng wèi zhè ge wèn tí fā chóu duō jiǔ le?
lines.english: How long have you been worrying about this?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 快两年了。我一直犹豫，不知道该放弃稳定还是坚持追求自己的理想。
lines.pinyin: Kuài liǎng nián le. Wǒ yī zhí yóu yù, bù zhī dào gāi fàng qì wěn dìng hái shì jiān chí zhuī qiú zì jǐ de lǐ xiǎng.
lines.english: Almost two years. I keep hesitating — I don't know whether to give up stability or keep pursuing my ideals.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我宁可选择一份有意义的工作，也不愿意在一份让自己后悔的工作上虚度时光。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ nìng kě xuǎn zé yī fèn yǒu yì yì de gōng zuò, yě bú yuàn yì zài yī fèn ràng zì jǐ hòu huǐ de gōng zuò shàng xū dù shí guāng.
lines.english: I would rather choose a meaningful job than waste my time in a job that I will come to regret.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 但我怕万一失败了，就真的后悔莫及了。
lines.pinyin: Dàn wǒ pà wàn yī shī bài le, jiù zhēn de hòu huǐ mò jí le.
lines.english: But I'm afraid that if I fail, it will be too late to regret it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 人生最大的遗憾，不是失败，而是从来没有去尝试。要有勇气，把握机会。
lines.pinyin: Rén shēng zuì dà de yí hàn, bú shì shī bài, ér shì cóng lái méi yǒu qù cháng shì. Yào yǒu yǒng qì, bǎ wò jī huì.
lines.english: The greatest regret in life is not failure, but never having tried. Have courage and seize the opportunity.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你觉得，一个人应该为了现实而妥协，还是坚持理想？
lines.pinyin: Nǐ jué de, yī gè rén yīng gāi wèi le xiàn shí ér tuǒ xié, hái shì jiān chí lǐ xiǎng?
lines.english: Do you think a person should compromise for the sake of reality, or hold on to their ideals?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 现实是不能忽视的。但一个人若没有理想，便只是在活着，而不是在生活。
lines.pinyin: Xiàn shí shì bù néng hū shì de. Dàn yī gè rén ruò méi yǒu lǐ xiǎng, biàn zhǐ shì zài huó zhe, ér bú shì zài shēng huó.
lines.english: Reality cannot be ignored. But a person without ideals is merely existing, not truly living.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 说得好。既然我们都知道理想的重要，就更应该为它而坚持，哪怕前途未知。
lines.pinyin: Shuō de hǎo. Jì rán wǒ men dōu zhī dào lǐ xiǎng de zhòng yào, jiù gèng yīng gāi wèi tā ér jiān chí, nǎ pà qián tú wèi zhī.
lines.english: Well said. Since we all know how important ideals are, we should persevere for them all the more — even if the future is uncertain.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 不过，现实和理想从来不是完全对立的。聪明的人知道如何在两者之间找到平衡。
lines.pinyin: Bú guò, xiàn shí hé lǐ xiǎng cóng lái bú shì wán quán duì lì de. Cōng míng de rén zhī dào rú hé zài liǎng zhě zhī jiān zhǎo dào píng héng.
lines.english: However, reality and ideals are never completely opposed. Wise people know how to find balance between the two.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 对。坚持理想，并不意味着忽视现实，而是在现实中找到通向理想的路。判断生活是否值得经历，本身就是在回答哲学的基本问题。
lines.pinyin: Duì. Jiān chí lǐ xiǎng, bìng bù yì wèi zhe hū shì xiàn shí, ér shì zài xiàn shí zhōng zhǎo dào tōng xiàng lǐ xiǎng de lù. Pànduàn shēng huó shìfǒu zhí dé jīng lì, běnshēn jiù shì zài huí dá zhé xué de jī běn wèn tí.
lines.english: Right. Holding onto ideals doesn't mean ignoring reality, but finding a path toward your ideals within reality. Judging whether life is worth living is itself an answer to a fundamental philosophical question.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 正是。理想本是无所谓有，无所谓无的。这正如地上的路；其实地上本没有路，走的人多了，也便成了路
lines.pinyin: Zhèng shì. Lǐ xiǎng běn shì wú suǒ wèi yǒu, wú suǒ wèi wú de. Zhè zhèng rú dì shàng de lù; qí shí dì shàng běn méi yǒu lù, zǒu de rén duō le, yě biàn chéng le lù.
lines.english: Exactly. Aspiration is neither something that exists nor something that doesn't exist. It's like a path on the ground; originally there was no path, but as more people walk on it, it becomes a path..
